message.content: Ferencz Dunki, master carpenter, married.
Ilona Szász, wife of Béla Puskás, a merchant.
Lajos Grúz, son of Albert Grúz, a Honvéd (soldier) and counsellor in a ministerial department; medical student.
Anna Ittlinger, wife of Ferencz Szedlakovics, a merchant from Eszék (Osijek).
Samu Dóczi, day laborer, married.
Rozália Bartók, wife of Károly Tóth, a maker of shoe lasts.
Etelka Mikó, daughter of Lőrincz Mikó, the shoemaker.
Tibor Keresztély, son of Lajos Keresztély, a teacher at the commercial (business) school.
Lászlóczki Dániel, Lászlóczki Dániel, the butcher's son.
Rozália Lengyel, wife of Sámuel Frank-Kis, the bell-ringer.
Terézia Horváth, wife of János Simonfi of Torda, a day-laborer.
Eszter Dávid, daughter of János Dávid, a day laborer.
István Bordi, born in Dombó, a second-year theology student.
Ilona Koncz, daughter of Mihály Koncz, a railway day laborer.

Ferenc Lakatos, son of Ferenc Lakatos, a farmer and fee‑collector.

(Note: "Ferencz" is an archaic spelling of "Ferenc"; "dijnok" likely means a collector of fees/taxes.)
Anna Bodnár, daughter of Mihály Bodnár, the wheelwright.
Margit Veres, daughter of Dr. Vilmos Veress, a teacher at a commercial school.
István Kovács, the shoemaker's unbaptized son.
Krisztina Kovács, daughter of István Kovács, the bootmaker.
Teréz Gyulai, female day laborer, unmarried.
Vilma Szeljes, wife of György Albert, the estate steward.
Mari Binetz, daughter of Mari Binetz, a housemaid (illegitimate)
Frankkis Gyula (the name was given to him by the midwife), Frankkis Sándor, son of a butcher, unbaptized.
Kriza Ferencz, Kriza Ferencz, a day-laborer's son.
Erzsi Kecskeméti, daughter of Elek Kecskeméti, a mason and churchwarden.
Victor Tóth, son of Károly Tóth, a maker of shoe lasts.
Róza Nagy, daughter of György Nagy, a theatre prompter.
Pál Endre, Pál Lina, the servant's son
József Binetz, furrier, unmarried
Albert Biró, born in Kidé, assistant baker, single.
Sándor Szilágyi, resident of Kolozsmonostor, son of József Szilágyi, a master carpenter.
János Ambrus, of Csehétfalva, a one‑year volunteer soldier, a student who graduated from our college in 1884/85.
János Radó, János Radó, the son of a day laborer.
József Pataki, son of Sámuel Pataki, an estate steward.
Mózes Ádám, born in Bözöd, 8th‑grade student.
Anna Nagy, widow of the late shoemaker János Techtig.
Klárika Benczédi, daughter of Gergely Benczédi, a grammar-school teacher.
Anna Ferencz, daughter of Bishop József Ferencz.
Villa Eszter — Mrs. József Kerekes, landlady.
Anna Kovács, the widow of Ferenc Balogh, Mrs. Csizmadia
József Biluska, József Biluska, the butcher's son.
Róza Huszár, domestic servant, born in Korond, unmarried.
Ilona Tamás, daughter of András Tamás, a railway day laborer.
Zsuzsánna Ferenczy, wife of Dénes Szigethyi, a former merchant.
Tordátfalvi Nemes József, lawyer, married.
Teréz Gyulai, daughter of the late tailor Zsigmond Gyulay.
Gyula Árpád Nenning-Várady, son of Antal Nenning-Várady and Anna Geringer Balogh.
Árpád Székely, son of the late Sándor Székely, a student in the gymnasium's sixth class.
Károlina Sala Enlaki, widow of the late Imre Gálfalvi, chief trustee.
József Márkus, son of Mari Márkus, a female tobacco‑factory worker.
András Kis, butcher, married.
Károly Várady, royal inspector of schools, meritorious teacher, and ecclesiastical councillor; married.
Róza Kelemen of Nagyernye, Oltszent in Háromszék, widow of the late Sámuel Beke.

Ilona Szabó, daughter of Ferencz Szabó, a tűzmester.

(Note: "tűzmester" is a rank — roughly an artillery/fire sergeant.)
Ilona Ferenczi, daughter of László Ferenczi, a mason.
Rózália Elcser, widow of György Losonczi (Mrs. Losonczi), a tailor's wife.
István Sárdi, István Sárdi, the teacher's son.
Lajos Lőrincz, son of György Lőrincz, a shoemaker.
Anonymous, son of Róza Szilágyi, a worker at a tobacco factory.
Ágnes Szigethi, the widow of the late Mihály Szélyes.
Zsuzsa Györke, daughter of János Györke, a sapper.
Ilona Albertini, daughter of Sámuel Albertini, a furrier.
Kálmán Kerestély, head of the financial directorate office; married.
Borbála Morvai, daughter of Ferenc Morvai, bootmaker.
Károly Telmán, assistant tailor, single (from Székelykeresztúr).
Juli Nagy, József Bálint’s mistress (born in Szentábrahám).
Anna Szász, born in Torockó; a maidservant working in Kolozsvár; illegitimate daughter of Anna Szász.
Károly Molnár, goldsmith’s assistant, unmarried
Zsuzsa Nagy was the divorced wife of Elek Kecskeméti, a church warden.
János Nemes, the illegitimate son of Nemes János, the theatre janitor.
Amália Dimény, wife of József Osváth, a grocer.

György Szöllősi, István Szöllősi (day laborer) and the son of Anna Pataki.

(Note: "fiok" in the original looks like a typo—likely meant "fia" = "son".)
Ágnes Gyulai, wife of carpenter Ferencz Haller.
Juliánna Kovács, widow of the late bell-ringer Mihály Pánti.
Samuel Molnár, button maker, married.
Gizella Veress, daughter of Dr. Vilmos Veress, a teacher at a commercial school.
Rozália K. Ajtai, wife of Antal Kovácsi, college professor.
Lajos Székely, assistant bookbinder, unmarried.
Róza Simon, daughter of István Simon, a court page.
Anna Árkosi, daughter of József Árkosi, a farmer.
Erzsi Ferenczi, daughter of István Ferenczi, a locomotive fireman.
Polixéna Kovács, wife of János Suchy, mechanic.
Irma Murvai, daughter of György Murvai, a café cashier.
Sándor Árkosi, son of József Árkosi, a farmer.
Székely Árpád, the illegitimate son of Székely Márton, a cobbler's assistant, and Kakas Borbála.
Mihály Rabszin, son of Márton Rabszin, a farmhand.
János Horváth, a married day laborer.
Árpád Kovács, son of János Kovács, college principal and teacher.
Róza Deák, an illegitimate daughter of Mihály Deák and Véri Bara, who were workers.
Anna Frankkis, daughter of Sándor Frankkis, a butcher.
Zsuzsa Albertfi, wife of József Tóth, the bootmaker.
András Kardos, son of Károly Kardos, a day laborer.
Zsuzsánna Bartha, widow of the late distinguished lawyer Ferencz Gyergyai.
György Papp, a married blacksmith born in Torockó.
József Sárdi, our congregation's cantor-teacher, is married.
Mária Horváth — a solitary, divorced woman.
Róza Frankkis, wife of Lajos Pap, the bootmaker.
Sándor Szőke, son of forestry inspector István Szőke.
Albert Fekete, son of István Fekete’s widow, shop assistant and Honvéd (private soldier).
Anna Radó, daughter of János Radó, a carpenter's assistant.
Katalin Szilvásy, widow of Miklós Szilvásy; daughter of Anna Gedő of Homorod‑Szentmárton.
Imre Gergely, the illegitimate son of Anna Gergely (of Toroczkósztgyörgy).
József Székely, the illegitimate son of Titi, a Szekler servant.
János Rabszin, son of Márton Rabszin, a farm worker.
Ödön Ambrus, son of Gergely Ambrus, telegraph operator — second-year gymnasium student.
Bálint Pongrátz, son of folk musician Lajos Pongrátz.
Erzsi Lakatos, the young daughter of Ferenc Lakatos, clerk.
Albert Péterfi, retired land registry officer, married.
Sándor Frankkis, butcher, married.
Ilona Molnár, daughter of Lajos Molnár, cellar master.
Ödön Biluska, son of József Biluska, a butcher.
Zsuzsa Gyulai, wife of master tailor Ferencz Kovács.
Etelka Lászlóczki, daughter of Dániel Lászlóczki, a butcher.
Lajos Molnár, winemaker, married.
Ilona Nanoszki, daughter of György Nanoszki, a carpenter.
Dániel Koncz, retired tax office accountant.
Katalin Daczó, wife of Domokos Rend, Royal Hungarian forester.
Ilona Ferenczi, daughter of László Ferenczi.
Erzsi Iszlai, daughter of Márton Iszlai, cantor and teacher.
Borbála Váradi, widow of the late János Reizner.
Borbára Széplaki Petrichevich Horváth, a woman of private means, unmarried.
Etelka Bodnár, daughter of Mihály Bodnár, a wheelwright.
Anna Bruzer, widow of the late József Váradi, bread baker.
Sándor Miklós Kénosi, chief notary of Hunyad County, married.
István Czakó — son of István Czakó, a shoemaker.

Anna Demeter, widow of Ignatz Elekes — Mrs. Szakács.

(Note: "szakácsné" literally means "Mrs. Szakács" or "the cook's wife.")
Kati Lula, the illegitimate daughter of master blacksmith György Lula and Mária Buzafi.
Juliánna Binecz, widow of the late furrier János Müller.
Zsuzsa Asztalos, wife of János Szilágyi, the gardener.
Áron Kovács, lawyer, widower, born in Algyő.
Albert Amálfi Albert László, son of the chief inspector of Szamosújvár, a student in the 4th grade of elementary school.
Mari Váradi, wife of István Fekete, the tailor.
Sándor Török, the illegitimate son of Vilmia Török, a resident of Ráczmilitics.
Viola Juli — unmarried domestic servant born in the village of Alsószentmihály.
Gyula Bartalis, son of Sándor Bartalis, a tanner.
Ágnes Katona, a domestic servant born in Kis-Solymos, unmarried.
Mihály Végh of Ádámos, a landowner in Magyarnádas, widower.
Anna Gedő, widow of the late Miklós Szilvási.
József Essig, a master chimney sweep born in Brassó, married.
Györke József, son of Györke János, a city sapper.
Dezső Solyom, son of Imre Solyom, shoemaker.
Judith Fancsali, widow of the late István Lőtei, church cleaner.
Margit Frankkis, daughter of the late butcher Sándor Frankkis.
Miklós Mikó, son of Miklós Mikó, judge of the Royal Court of Appeal; seventh‑year gymnasium (high‑school) student.
Eszter Lévi, wife of Zoltán Gyula, the landowner of Csomafáj.
Endre Cservenka, born in Marianupol, Galicia, official of the Hungarian State Railways.
János Kovács, son of János Kovács, headmaster of the main gymnasium.
Borbála Rácz, wife of Pál Árkosi, a farmer.
Zsuzsa Rabszin, daughter of Márton Rabszin, a farmer.
Lajos Pataki, a bootmaker, is married.
József Pataki, shoemaker, married.
Mihály Incze (of Nagyajta), retired ministerial secretary; unmarried.
József Váradi, a master tinsmith, is married.
István Bartók, medical scholar and teacher, single.
Mária Sinczki, wife of Antal Szabó, a hairdresser.
Honoreta Izsák, a domestic servant born in Kadács, unmarried.
Mihály Pál, the illegitimate son of Anna Pál, a nurse.
Ilona Biluska, daughter of József Biluska, a butcher.
György Kúti, the illegitimate son of Mari Kuti, a female tobacco‑factory worker.
Radó János. Radó János, the day-laborer's child.
János Farkas is the son of Mihály Farkas, a newspaper deliveryman.
Róza Rabszin, daughter of Márton Rabszin, a farmer.
Mari Theil, daughter of János Theil, carpenter.
Juli Hosszú, daughter of János Hosszú, a miller.
Róza Theil, daughter of János Theil, a carpenter.
Móricz Ferencz, son of Moricz Ferencz, a teacher.
Máté Albert — Máté Albert, the mason's son.
Anna Tóth, the child of Márton Tóth, a day laborer.
Gyula Ajtai, unmarried butcher.
Zsuzsánna Simó, widow of the late József Forray, a female day laborer.
György Kézsmárki, butcher's assistant, unmarried.
János Danka, a court page, born in Sissolymos.
Lina Jakab, daughter of Mihály Jakab, a farmer.
Kálmán Kozma, son of Ferenc Kozma, royal school inspector.
András Kardos, a wheelwright from Torda County, born in Szingfalva.

József Benker, stage machinist, husband of Marcella Molncha.

(Note: "Molncha" looks like it may be a typo in the original; possibly "Molnár".)
Anna Blóz, daughter of János Blóz, a bootmaker — a female worker at a tobacco factory.
Ferenc Móricz — at our shooting school; husband of elementary-school teacher Ilona Bakós.
Mária Váradi, wife of József Kovács, a day laborer.
Ferenc Rend, son of Domokos Rend, royal Hungarian forester (Dobrest).
Anna Fehér, daughter of Tivadar Fehér and Zsuzsa Perdéli (Ar. Gyéres)
Dezső Orbán, the illegitimate son of Anna Orbán.
Gábriella Hort, daughter of József Hort, railway engineer (Nagyvárad).

István Pálfi, Pálfi Zsuzsa's illegitimate son.

(Note: I corrected the likely typo "tövénytelen" → "törvénytelen" meaning "illegitimate". If you meant a different punctuation/relationship, tell me and I can adjust.)
József Jancsó, retired municipal deputy captain, married.
György Tálas, born in Kide, unmarried, stove-maker.
Béla Szentmártony, husband of Laura Breitmayer, telegraph officer in Arad.
Mózes Szabó of Homorodalmás, secretary at the Royal Hungarian Ministry of Finance, unmarried.
Anna Osváth, widow of Gergely Kovács, grocer.
György Móricz was the son of the late teacher Ferencz Móricz.
András Bartha, husband of Magdolna Kornya, mining landowner of Verespatak and churchwarden.
Adám Borbála, Ádám Biri's illegitimate daughter.
Lajos Balogh, son of customs inspector György Balogh.
Erzsi Bartalis, daughter of Sándor Bartalis, a railway guard.
Ida Szathmári, daughter of Ferencz Szathmári, shoemaker.
Kriza Lenka, the wife of János Kovács, a college director and teacher.
János Kromer, a herdsman born in Kövend, unmarried.
Nagyernyei Kelemen Elek, landowner of Szucság, unmarried.
Jolán Máthé — the illegitimate child of Rebeka Máthé, a servant.
Szentkirállyi Ferencz — the unbaptized son of Szentkirállyi Ferencz, Royal Hungarian Honvéd captain.
Árpád Osváth, actor; son of Gerő Osváth, a qualified lawyer; unmarried.
Dezső Murvai, private, the unbaptized son of György Murvai.
Dániel Para, born in Árkos, a day laborer, single.
István Demény, son of István Demény, a shop assistant, student in the second class of the gymnasium.
Lajos Paró, son of János Paró, a shop assistant; student in the 5th elementary class.
Géza Hercze, the illegitimate son of Sára Hercze, a cook.
Áron Benedek, teacher at the women's training school; married.
Katalin Tóth, cook, unmarried.
Márton Gyöngyösi, son of István Gyöngyösi, a worker.
Gyula Székely, son of József Székely, a mail carrier.
Zsuzsa Jakab, daughter of Mihály Jakab, the carter.
Frankkis, a bell-ringer, son of Sámuel Frankkis, a private (rank-and-file) soldier.
Péter Lászloczky, town citizen, master butcher and church councillor, married.
Vilma Kardos, daughter of Károly Kardos, a day laborer.
Berta Pongrácz, daughter of Lajos Pongrácz, a folk musician.
József Farkas, son of Vilmos Farkas, a merchant.
Sándor Kézsmárki, carpenter, married.
Árpád Veress, city district physician; child of Dr. Lajos Veress.
József Árkosi, son of Pál Árkosi, a railway day laborer.
Imre Sólyom, son of Imre Sólyom, a shoemaker.
Emma Orosz, Kati Orosz's illegitimate child.
Juli Katonai, daughter of Mari Katonai, the housemaid.
Unbaptized boy. The unbaptized son of Endre Takács, railway conductor.
Samu Hosszú, son of János Hosszú, the miller.
József Kovács, son of Miklós Kovács, the shoemaker.
Juliánna Jelen, of independent means, unmarried.
Eszter Toroczkai, daughter of György Toroczkai, a police officer.

Literal: "Ferenczi Ilona — daughter of Ferenczi István v. főtisz‑titó."

Natural/interpreted (likely): "Ilona Ferenczi, daughter of István Ferenczi, vitéz, senior officer."

Note: "v." is probably the abbreviation "vitéz" (a Hungarian honorific) and "főtisz‑..." appears to be a hyphenated/garbled form of "főtiszt" (senior officer). If you can supply the original source image or confirm the abbreviated word, I can give a more certain rendering.
Ferenc Fridl, son of Ferenc Fridl (called "Pir"), a file‑cutter.
Margit Bodnár, daughter of Mihály Bornár, a wheelwright.
Anna Kecskeméthi, widow of József Bede, living on private means.
Székely Ádám, Royal Hungarian financial official, unmarried.
Várnay Győző, husband of Irén Karl, municipal engineer in Hajdúszoboszló.
Lajos Balogh, son of Gyögy Balogh, a customs inspector.
Abrudbányai Abrudbányay Béla, a landscape gardener born in Szászsebes, unmarried.
Sámuel Sebe, lawyer, widower.
János Beczkoy, actor, married.
Béla Abrudbányai Abrudbányay, husband of Betti Engländer, Royal Hungarian forestry director.
Mária Tálas, daughter of Lázár Tálas, a stove-factory worker.
Zsuzsa Lakatos, daughter of Ferencz Lakatos, deputy city auditor.
János Csegezy, customs officer, born in Udvarhely and residing in Dicsőszentmárton; married.
Anikó Tunyogi, from Torda, wife of Áron Nagy.
Anna Horváth, daughter of András Horváth, a farmer.
Juliánna Mózes, widow of the late farmer Márton Kovács.
György Szönyi, son of Gábor Szönyi.
Gyula Kriza, husband of Gizella Arringer, secretary at the Royal Hungarian Finance Directorate.
Mihály Dénes, landowner of Sófalva, born in Derzs, Udvarhely County — widower.
Juliánna Tátiás, Mrs. József Réz — without occupation.
Mátyás Sánta, a native of Alsósiménfalva, day laborer.
Gyula Mester is the son of György Mester, a railway porter.
Albert Kriza, son of Ferenc Kriza, a butcher.
Péter Török of Csekefalva, husband of Anna Farkas, sworn attorney and retired district judge of Szamosújvár.
Julianna Jakab, wife of Gyöngyösi Jakab Márton, a farmer.
Kovácsi Antal, a highly respected teacher and parish administrative clerk.
Terézia Gedő of Homoródszentmárton, widow of the late Elek Kaáli‑Nagy, ministerial councillor and chief church trustee.
József Szabó, a native of Szabolcs County, born in Ibrány, unmarried, merchant, Debrecen, Nagy Hatvan Street.
Radó Samu, day laborer, single.
Dr. Áron Berde of Laborfalva, husband of Károlina Szarvády, retired university professor and chief churchwarden.
Mihály Tóth, son of teacher Mihály Tóth — Nagyvárad, Nyárfa Alley, No. 432.
Lajos Serly, son of the conductor Lajos Serly.
Mária Lászlóczky, head of the office at the Royal Finance Directorate; wife of Ferenc Kondász.
Unbaptized boy, Szél Zsuzsa, the maid's son.
Pálfy Lajos, a native of Kis-Illye, a student in the sixth class of the gymnasium.

Miklós Czakó
Miklós Czakó, the postman's child
Erzsi Iszlai, daughter of Márton Iszlai, a cantor and teacher.
Ádám Ákos, an illegitimate child.
Berta Iszlai, daughter of Márton Iszlai, cantor-teacher.
Erzsi Szabó, daughter of police officer Sándor Szabó.
János Paget, born in England, a widower and large landowner in Gyera and Kolozsvár.
Gyuri Halán, Hungarian, born in Sáros, day laborer, married.
Klárisz Nagy, daughter of István Nagy, county clerk.
Károly Olgyay, son of János Olgyay, director of the institute for the deaf and mute.
Juliánna Kovácsi, widow of the late Sándor Binecz.
Zsuzsa Deák, daughter of Mihály Deák, a day laborer.
Mariska Iszlai, daughter of cantor and schoolteacher Márton Iszlai.
Béla Varga, the son of tax inspector Mór Varga.
István Dezső, the unbaptized son of János Dezső, a farmer.
Lajos Berde, son of Lajos Berde, shoemaker.
István Szabó, native and resident of the locality, widower, mason.
Lajos Szabó, a landowner from Apahida, married.
Langer Ferencz, husband of Rebeka Tamási, insurance clerk.
Terézia Székely, wife of the late Lajos Pákey, secretary of the Supreme Government Council.
Béla Szentmártoni, son of Béla Szentmártoni and Laura Breitmayer, commercial apprentice.
Anna Halász, the wife of Imre Ságár, a shoemaker.
Pál Farkas, son of Vilmos Farkas, a merchant.
Ágnes Bruzer, the widow of the late carpenter Zsigmond Gyulai.
Sándor Serly, the son of Lajos Serly, a theatre conductor.
János Hajós, son of Gyula Hajós, a railway engineer.
Irma Borsos, daughter of the late merchant Imre Borsos.

István Árkosi, son of József Árkosi, farmer.

(Note: "földész" can also be rendered as "landholder/landowner" depending on context.)
Teréz Jánosi, wife of Ferenc Szabó, the confectioner.
Mihály Antal, butcher, single.
Zsuzsa Horváth, the daughter of András Horváth, a day laborer.
Anna Miklós, daughter of József Miklós, a coachman.
István Leckmüller (Kromer), single, carpenter's assistant.
Zsigmond Gothárd, landowner of Sztána, widower.
Janosi Gerő, husband of Ráchel Gedő, retired civil servant.
Albert Végh, son of Liza Végh.
István Györke, son of János Györke, a city sapper.
Zsuzsánna Tóth, widow of the late tanner Mihály Farkas.
Vizoly Zakariás, husband of Mária Imhof, state high-school teacher.
Erzsi Bálint, daughter of András Bálint, the bootmaker.
István Máthé, husband of Mária Ádám, tailor.
Róza Kézsmárki, widow of the late György Kis, day laborer.
Ilona Haraték, daughter of Ferencz Haraték, stonemason.
János Lázár, an unmarried teacher born in Szentábrahám.
Márton Tobiás, son of Ida Tobiás.
Anna Szöllősi, the wife of Márton Kilin, a day laborer.
Ella Puskás, daughter of Béla Puskás.
Krisztina Ötvös, daughter of Anna Ötvös.
Anna Mezei, widow of György Binetz, kifli seller (crescent-roll vendor).
Sándor Vernes, born in Torockó, a student at the training school.
András Kis, a cobbler and customs collector born in Sepsi‑Szentkirály, married.
Judith Somai, the wife of Zsigmond Szabó, a railway stoker.
János König, cloth dyer (born in Pöltenberg), married.
Zsuzsánna Csiszér, wife of János Radó, a vineyard worker.
Anna Döngölő, the wife of Péter Lakatos, a day laborer.
János Bányai, a landowner from Középlak, married.
Laborfalvi Berde Mózes, retired counselor of the ministerial department, honorary trustee of our church.
An unbaptized girl, the child of János Hantz, a bookbinder.
Miklós Pálfy, son of György Pálfy, an estate steward.
János Szöllösi, son of János Szölösi, a railway trackman.
Biri Pálfi (Mrs. Farkas Szász), a woman born in Várfalva.
Ádám Biri's daughter, born in Kolozsvár.
Antal Fülöp, son of Miklós Fülöp, a railway brakeman.
Gyula Székely, son of Károly Székely, a prison guard.
Mária Adorjáni, daughter of Ferencz Adorjáni, a railway guard at Rév.
Aranka Repeczky, wife of Péter Vertán, in Arad, 31 years old; peritonitis; died Dec. 19. Funeral officiant: Pastor Csécsi.
Anna Killyén, widow of József Puskás of Debrő.
Ferencz Bokor, son of István Bokor, a coachman.
